last_action: PR #164 opened — fix(spec-viewer): show disabled "Generating…" footer until step artifact lands

T001.did: Created stepArtifact.ts with hasNonTrivialArtifact() — maps step→md file, strips frontmatter, requires a heading or ≥40 non-ws chars; missing/empty/stub → false.

T002.did: Added runningStepArtifactReady + runningStepStartedAt to extension NavState.

T003.did: Mirrored the two readiness fields in webview NavState and added the markStepComplete message variant.

T004.did: Added loading prop to shared Button — renders .btn-spinner and forces disabled.

T005.did: Added .btn-spinner + rotation keyframes and .is-loading style to _buttons.css (plan said _actions.css; real footer/button partials are _footer.css/_buttons.css).
concerns: Plan referenced _actions.css which does not exist; spinner placed in _buttons.css to match the Button component.

T006.did: Provider derives the raw running step + startedAt and computes runningStepArtifactReady (via hasNonTrivialArtifact; implement uses taskCompletionPercent>=100), shipping both on the contentUpdated navState.

T007.did: Added markStepComplete message handler — resolves the running step from .spec-context.json stepHistory and calls completeStep, then refreshes.

T008.did: FooterActions early-returns a disabled 'Generating <step>…' (loading) button + 'Mark step complete' fallback while running && !artifactReady && !timedOut; useEffect timer flips after a 10-min recovery window; changed visible=vs.footer so the footer shows enabled once ready.
concerns: Legacy fallback footer branch (vs.footer empty) still hides on isRunning — left as-is since SDD specs always populate vs.footer.

T009.did: Added stepArtifact.test.ts — 9 cases covering heading/body/empty/whitespace/frontmatter-only/stub/missing/implement; all green.

T010.did: Rewrote the viewer-states 'Hide-during-in-flight' note as 'Generating-during-in-flight' (disabled spinner button, content-aware completion, manual button, 10-min timeout).

T011.did: Updated README 'Quiet, intentional footer' bullet to describe the disabled Generating state, content-based re-enable, manual completion, and recovery timeout.

build.did: npm run compile + webview tsc --noEmit clean; npm test → 50 suites / 560 tests pass.

T012.did: CP1 follow-up — added Storybook coverage for the Generating state: updated inFlightNavState (recent startedAt + runningStepArtifactReady:false) so in-flight stories render Generating, plus dedicated Generating Tasks/Plan, artifact-ready, and recovery-timeout stories. Live on http://localhost:6006.

T013.did: Folded-in unrelated fix: optional refinement commands (Clarify/Checklist/Analyze) now hidden once the footer offers complete/reactivate (closure-ready), gating on actual footer actions rather than the status string. Added a suppression story + README note.
concerns: Unrelated to the spec-099 scope; folded into this PR at the user's request.

concerns.note: Plan referenced _actions.css which does not exist; spinner placed in _buttons.css to match the Button component.
Legacy fallback footer branch (vs.footer empty) still hides on isRunning — left as-is since SDD specs always populate vs.footer.

approach: Replace the footer's hide-while-running behavior with a content-aware Generating state, driven by a filesystem artifact check the provider passes to the webview.

specify.key_finding: Footer next-step button advances optimistically — handleApprove (messageHandlers.ts) calls completeStep+startStep then dispatches the CLI before the artifact exists; the current design hides the footer during in-flight rather than disabling it. File watchers (fileWatchers.ts) already refresh the viewer on *.md create/change, so completion can be detected without new polling.

plan.approach_summary: Replace the footer's hide-while-running behavior with a content-aware Generating state, driven by a filesystem artifact check the provider passes to the webview.
risks: Re-introducing file-existence as a completion signal partially reverses spec-060 — scope it to the button render state only, not the canonical badge/status derivation.
Recovery-timeout window: too short strands fast machines, too long hides silent AI failures — generous default plus the manual button covers the gap.
